examples.title: Basic Signal Handler Setup
examples.description: Demonstrates how to set up a basic signal handler for SIGINT using signal() function
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates basic signal handling. When user presses Ctrl+C (SIGINT), instead of terminating immediately, the custom handler executes, allowing graceful cleanup. The signal() function sets the disposition - associating a handler function with a specific signal.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - constant time for handler invocation
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - minimal stack usage for handler

examples.title: Signal Handling with sigaction
examples.description: More robust signal handling using POSIX sigaction() with SA_RESTART flag
examples.explanation: This code uses sigaction() which is more reliable than signal(). Key features: (1) sa_mask specifies signals blocked during handler execution, (2) SA_RESTART flag automatically restarts interrupted system calls, (3) Allows finer control over signal handling behavior.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - constant space for sigaction structure

examples.title: Handling Multiple Signals with Signal Mask
examples.description: Demonstrates blocking signals and checking pending signals using sigprocmask and sigpending
examples.explanation: This demonstrates: (1) sigprocmask() to block/unblock signals, (2) sigpending() to check which signals are pending, (3) raise() to send signal to current process. When SIGUSR1 is blocked, the signal becomes pending. When unblocked, the handler is invoked.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - constant space for signal sets
